mbta_alerts.header: The platform at Jackson Square is undergoing renovations to improve safety and accessibility. Sections of the platform are unlevel, which may make it difficult for some riders to board and exit trains.
Green Line B Branch: Shuttle buses are replacing service between Kenmore and Boston College through Thursday, April 30, for infrastructure upgrades.

On Monday, March 30, a construction schedule will go into effect on the Newburyport/Rockport Line. Between 9 AM and 3 PM on weekdays, 4 inbound and 4 outbound trains will be temporarily suspended. Visit MBTA.com/CR for more information.

The stairway connecting the Jackson Square lobby and the south end of the platform is closed until winter 2026. Use the stairway at the north end of the platform. This closure is for the construction of a new stairway and a second elevator.

Green Line E: Service will be suspended between Government Center and Heath Street from Sat, May 30 through Sat, Jun 6 for infrastructure upgrades. Regular B, C and D Branch service will operate during this work.

There is no accessible path connecting the inbound and outbound platforms at Wellesley Square Station. The existing path includes steep slopes and is long. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

Blue Line: Service will be suspended between Airport and Bowdoin the weekend of Sat, May 9 and Sun, May 10 due to maintenance. Free and accessible shuttle buses will make stops at Airport, Maverick, and Haymarket.
Green Line C Branch: Shuttle buses will replace service between Kenmore and Cleveland Circle from Wed, May 6 through Sun, May 17, for infrastructure upgrades.
Littleton/Route 495 passengers can expect occasional traffic and detours accessing the station due to the Foster Street reconstruction work.
Please board all Framingham/Worcester Line trains on the inbound platform (Track 2) at Wellesley Farms through West Natick until further notice today.
To accommodate Providence Station Renovations, construction barriers have been installed and the Southern stairwell to Track 1 is out of service. Staircase access to Track 3 and Track 5 are now open.
Mattapan Line: Shuttle buses replace service between Ashmont and Mattapan beginning at 6 PM on Thursday, May 14, until the end of service on Saturday, May 16, for maintenance work.
The Riverside Ave entrance at Campello Station is currently closed. Passengers may continue to use the Plain St entrance.

Red Line: Shuttle buses replace service between Broadway, Quincy Center, and Ashmont from May 29 - May 31, due to signal work. Commuter Rail service will be fare-free between South Station and Braintree during this work.
Red Line: Shuttle buses replace service between Broadway and Ashmont beginning at 6:00 PM on Thursday, May 14 through Monday, May 18, for signal work.

Red Line: Shuttle buses replace service between Broadway and Quincy Center from Saturday, May 2 through Monday, May 4, for signal work. Commuter Rail service will be fare-free between South Station and Braintree during this work.

news_revere.title: Mayor Keefe Joins Revere Leaders to Announce “Field of Play,” A Series of World Cup Watch Parties
news_revere.summary:
REVERE, MA – Amidst lively youth pickup soccer games, the City of Revere and Mayor Patrick M. Keefe Jr. today joined with local business and civic leaders at Rumney Marsh Academy to announce the City’s “Field of Play” summer tourism initiative, inviting residents and visitors to share in the excitement of the World Cup across every corner of the city.
 
“As a city that has more than a quarter of our residents hailing from countries across Latin America, North Africa, and Europe, the number of international soccer fans in Revere is unmatched,” said Mayor Patrick M. Keefe Jr. “As a community that is rich with diversity, we are going to have a series of free events that puts our culture and our business in the spotlight. This is about collaboration in excitement for FIFA, and strong partnerships between our public and private entities,” added Mayor Keefe.
 
Field of Play is a tourism-driven events series built around free FIFA World Cup watch parties and events – highlighting the City of Revere’s diverse community and culture; providing no-cost, safe places for families from across the region (and world) to enjoy the fun of the World Cup; and driving foot traffic to small business districts in Revere.
 
Joe Gravellese, City of Revere Community Development Planner and co-chair of the Field of Play Planning Committee, commented, “Revere Beach has long been a hub for fans celebrating the World Cup and this year will be no different. We are thrilled to plan a summer of events for fans of all ages and backgrounds to celebrate this tournament, and to promote Revere’s vibrant small business community.”
 
The City’s full suite of programming will include free public watch parties for marquee matches; partnerships with local bars, restaurants, and small businesses hosting their own tournament celebrations; family and recreational programming; and placemaking and public art that honor both the global game and the many cultures that call Revere home. At today’s press conference, the Mayor was joined by partners including Revere Chamber of Commerce President Don Martelli, Doug Manz, Partner, Chief Investment Officer at The HYM Investment Group, Shirley Ave Neighborhood Association member Josué Vides Erazo, Cesar Salazar, Director of Revere FC Bolts, and representatives from Revere’s small businesses, including Murray’s Tavern and Twisted Fate Brewing.
 
Don Martelli, President of the Revere Chamber of Commerce and Field of Play community partner, noted, “This partnership puts Revere’s small businesses at the center of a global moment. When fans come to our districts for these events, they’re not just watching the World Cup — they’re discovering the restaurants, shops, and neighborhoods that make Revere unlike anywhere else. That’s the kind of exposure you can’t buy, and our members are ready to welcome the world.”
 
Fans will have the opportunity to celebrate the tournament on America's First Public Beach, along the walkable and culturally rich Shirley Ave corridor, in the emerging Beachmont Square neighborhood at Suffolk Downs, and in Revere's downtown. Each neighborhood will offer a unique matchday experience.
 
“The HYM Investment Group is proud to partner with Mayor Keefe and the City of Revere to make this an unforgettable summer at Suffolk Downs and Beachmont Square. With FIFA World Cup watch parties at The Yard,alongside incredible local businesses like Twisted Fate Brewery and The Point Barre and Yoga, we are excited to welcome residents and visitors from across the region to experience everything this community has to offer. Revere has always been a place that welcomes the world, and this summer we get to prove it,” said Doug Manz, Partner, Chief Investment Officer, The HYM Investment Group, LLC.
 
“Murray’s Tavern is very excited to be partners of Revere’s Field of Play Initiative. This collaborative teamwork along with Revere’s leaders and the Chamber of Commerce will create once in a lifetime memories for all ages! The excitement is at an all time high, and we will proudly showcase a taste of Murray’s diverse team, culinary flavor, and libations to both our neighbors and the world,” added John Murray, owner of Murray’s Tavern.
 
Field of Play Schedule of Events
 
USA World Cup Kickoff Party
  • Friday, June 12, 2026 | 3:00PM - 12:00AM
  • Waterfront Square (Dryft)
  • Canada vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina, USA vs. Paraguay
2026 World Cup Kickoff Festival @ Suffolk Downs
  • Saturday, June 13, 2026 | 3:00PM - 9:00PM
  • The Yard @ Suffolk Downs
  • Qatar vs. Switzerland, Brazil vs. Morocco, Haiti vs. Scotland
2026 World Cup Festival and Ciclovia @ Shirley Avenue
  • Friday, June 19, 2026 | 3:00PM - 9:00PM
  • Shirley Avenue
  • USA vs. Australia, Scotland vs. Morocco, Brazil vs. Haiti
Field of Play Youth Soccer Bonanza
  • Wednesday, June 24, 2026 | 3:00PM - 9:00PM (final day of Revere Public Schools)
  • Harry Della Russo Stadium
  • Matchups to be confirmed 
2026 World Cup Festival and Colombia Rally @ Suffolk Downs
*Revere has second largest Colombian population in the state

  • Suffolk Downs

El alcalde Keefe se une a los líderes de Revere para anunciar la serie de fiestas para ver la Copa del Mundo llamada «Field of Play»

La iniciativa turística de verano para los aficionados al fútbol incluirá actividades y eventos gratuitos en la primera playa pública de Estados Unidos y cerca de los centros de transporte de la MBTA

REVERE, MA – En medio de animados partidos improvisados de fútbol juvenil, la ciudad de Revere y el alcalde Patrick M. Keefe Jr. se han reunido hoy con líderes empresariales y cívicos locales en la Escuela Rumney Marsh para anunciar la iniciativa turística de verano de la ciudad llamada «Field of Play», invitando a residentes y visitantes a compartir la emoción del Mundial en todos los rincones de la ciudad.

“Como ciudad en la que más de una cuarta parte de nuestros residentes proceden de países de América Latina, el norte de África y Europa, el número de aficionados al fútbol internacional en Revere no tiene parangón», afirmó el alcalde Patrick M. Keefe Jr. "Como comunidad con una gran diversidad, vamos a celebrar una serie de eventos gratuitos que pondrán nuestra cultura y nuestro comercio en el punto de mira. Se trata de colaborar en el entusiasmo por la FIFA y de establecer sólidas alianzas entre nuestras entidades públicas y privadas", añadió el alcalde Keefe.

Campo de juego es una serie de eventos impulsados por el turismo que gira en torno a fiestas y eventos gratuitos para ver la Copa Mundial de la FIFA, con el fin de destacar la diversidad comunitaria y cultural de la ciudad de Revere; proporcionar lugares seguros y gratuitos para que las familias de toda la región (y del mundo) disfruten de la diversión de la Copa Mundial; y atraer visitantes a nuestros distritos de pequeños negocios en Revere

Joe Gravellese, planificador de desarrollo comunitario de la ciudad de Revere y copresidente del Comité de Planificación de Campo de juego, comentó: "La Playa ha sido durante mucho tiempo un punto de encuentro para los aficionados que celebran la Copa del Mundo y este año no será diferente. Estamos encantados de planificar un verano de eventos para que aficionados de todas las edades y procedencias celebren este torneo, y de promover la vibrante comunidad de pequeños negocios de Revere".

El programa completo de la ciudad incluirá fiestas públicas gratuitas para ver los partidos más destacados; colaboraciones con bares, restaurantes y pequeños comercios locales que organizarán sus propias celebraciones del torneo; actividades familiares y recreativas; y proyectos de embellecimiento urbano y arte público que rinden homenaje tanto a

el fútbol mundial y las numerosas culturas que tienen su hogar en Revere. En la rueda de prensa de hoy, el alcalde estuvo acompañado por varios colaboradores, entre ellos Don Martelli, presidente de la Cámara de Comercio de Revere; Doug Manz, socio y director de inversiones de The HYM Investment Group; Josué Vides Erazo, miembro de la Asociación de Vecinos de Shirley Ave; César Salazar, director del Revere FC Bolts; y representantes de pequeñas empresas de Revere, como Murray’s Tavern y Twisted Fate Brewing.

Don Martelli, presidente de la Cámara de Comercio de Revere y socio comunitario de Campo de Juego, señaló: "Esta colaboración sitúa a las pequeñas empresas de Revere en el centro de un momento mundial. Cuando los aficionados vengan a nuestros barrios para estos eventos, no solo estarán viendo el Mundial, sino que descubrirán los restaurantes, las tiendas y los barrios que hacen de Revere un lugar único. Ese es el tipo de exposición que no se puede comprar, y nuestros miembros están listos para dar la bienvenida al mundo"

Los aficionados tendrán la oportunidad de celebrar el torneo en la primera playa pública de Estados Unidos, a lo largo del corredor peatonal y culturalmente rico de Shirley Ave, en el emergente barrio de Beachmont Square en Suffolk Downs y en el centro de Revere. Cada barrio ofrecerá una experiencia única los días de partido.

"El HYM Investment Group se enorgullece de asociarse con el alcalde Keefe y la ciudad de Revere para hacer de este un verano inolvidable en Suffolk Downs y Beachmont Square. Con las fiestas para ver la Copa Mundial de la FIFA en The Yard, junto a increíbles negocios locales como Twisted Fate Brewery y The Point Barre and Yoga, estamos encantados de dar la bienvenida a residentes y visitantes de toda la región para que disfruten de todo lo que esta comunidad tiene que ofrecer. Revere siempre ha sido un lugar que da la bienvenida al mundo, y este verano vamos a demostrarlo", afirmó Doug Manz, socio y director de inversiones de The HYM Investment Group, LLC.

"Murray’s Tavern está muy emocionada por ser socia de la iniciativa Campo de juego de Revere. ¡Este trabajo en equipo colaborativo junto con los líderes de Revere y la Cámara de Comercio creará recuerdos únicos para todas las edades! La emoción está por las nubes, y mostraremos con orgullo una muestra del equipo diverso de Murray’s, sus sabores culinarios y sus bebidas tanto a nuestros vecinos como al mundo", añadió John Murray, propietario de Murray’s Tavern.

Calendario de eventos de Campo de juego

Fiesta de inauguración de la Copa del Mundo de EE. UU.

· Viernes, 12 de junio de 2026 | 3:00 PM- 12:00 AM

· Waterfront Square (Dryft)

· Canadá contra Bosnia y Herzegovina, EE. UU. contra Paraguay

Prefeito Keefe se junta aos líderes de Revere para anunciar "Field of Play", uma série de festas para assistir à Copa do Mundo  

A iniciativa de turismo de verão para fãs de futebol incluirá atividades e eventos gratuitos na Primeira Praia Pública da América e próximos aos centros da MBTA 

REVERE, MA – Em meio a animados jogos de futebol informal para jovens, a cidade de Revere e o prefeito Patrick M. Keefe Jr. se uniram hoje a líderes empresariais e cívicos locais da Rumney Marsh Academy para anunciar a iniciativa de turismo de verão "Field of Play" da cidade, convidando moradores e visitantes a compartilharem a empolgação da Copa do Mundo em todos os cantos da cidade. 

"Como uma cidade que tem mais de um quarto dos nossos habitantes vindos de países da América Latina, Norte da África e Europa, o número de torcedores internacionais de futebol em Revere é incomparável", disse o prefeito Patrick M. Keefe Jr. "Como uma comunidade rica em diversidade, vamos realizar uma série de eventos gratuitos que colocam nossa cultura e nossos negócios em destaque. Trata-se de colaboração em empolgação pela FIFA e de fortes parcerias entre nossas entidades públicas e privadas", acrescentou o prefeito Keefe. 

Field of Play é uma série de eventos orientados ao turismo, construída em torno de festas e eventos gratuitos da Copa do Mundo FIFA – destacando a diversidade da comunidade e cultura da cidade de Revere; oferecendo espaços seguros e gratuitos para famílias de toda a região (e do mundo) aproveitarem a diversão da Copa do Mundo; e atrair o fluxo de pedestres para nossos pequenos distritos comerciais em Revere 

Joe Gravellese, Planejador de Desenvolvimento Comunitário da Cidade de Revere e co-presidente do Comitê de Planejamento do Campo de Jogo, comentou: "A Praia de Revere há muito tempo é um ponto de encontro para os torcedores que celebram a Copa do Mundo e este ano não será diferente. Estamos muito felizes em planejar um verão de eventos para fãs de todas as idades e origens, para celebrar este torneio e promover a vibrante comunidade de pequenas empresas da Revere." 

A programação completa da cidade incluirá festas públicas gratuitas para assistir a partidas de destaque; parcerias com bares, restaurantes e pequenos negócios locais que organizam suas próprias celebrações de torneios; programação familiar e recreativa; e criação de lugares e arte pública que homenageiam tanto o futebol global quanto as muitas culturas que chamam Revere de lar. Na coletiva de imprensa de hoje, o prefeito foi acompanhado por sócios, incluindo o presidente da Câmara de Comércio de Revere, Don Martelli, Doug Manz, sócio, diretor de investimentos do The HYM Investment Group, Josué Vides Erazo, membro da Shirley Ave Neighborhood Association, Cesar Salazar, diretor do Revere FC Bolts, e representantes dos pequenos negócios da Revere, incluindo Murray's Tavern e Twisted Fate Brewing. 

Don Martelli, presidente da Câmara de Comércio de Revere e parceiro comunitário do Field of Play, observou: "Essa parceria coloca os pequenos negócios da Revere no centro de um momento global. Quando os fãs vêm aos nossos distritos para esses eventos, eles não estão apenas assistindo à Copa do Mundo — eles estão descobrindo os restaurantes, lojas e bairros que tornam Revere diferente de qualquer outro lugar. Esse é o tipo de exposição que você não pode comprar, e nossos membros estão prontos para receber o mundo." 

Os fãs terão a oportunidade de celebrar o torneio na Primeira Praia Pública da América, ao longo do corredor caminhável e culturalmente rico da Shirley Ave, no emergente bairro Beachmont Square em Suffolk Downs e no centro da cidade de Revere. Cada bairro oferecerá uma experiência única em dia de jogo. 

"O HYM Investment Group tem orgulho de fazer parceria com o prefeito Keefe e a cidade de Revere para tornar este um verão inesquecível em Suffolk Downs e Beachmont Square. Com festas para assistir à Copa do Mundo FIFA no The Yard, além de incríveis negócios locais como a Twisted Fate Brewery e a The Point Barre and Yoga, estamos entusiasmados em receber moradores e visitantes de toda a região para vivenciar tudo o que esta comunidade tem a oferecer. Revere sempre foi um lugar que acolhe o mundo, e neste verão vamos provar isso", disse Doug Manz, sócio e diretor de investimentos da The HYM Investment Group, LLC. 

 

"A Taverna Murray está muito animada em ser parceira da Iniciativa Campo de Revere. Esse trabalho em equipe colaborativo, junto com os líderes da Revere e a Câmara de Comércio, criará memórias únicas na vida para todas as idades! A empolgação está no auge, e vamos mostrar com orgulho um gostinho da equipe diversificada de Murray, do sabor culinário e das bebidas para nossos vizinhos e para o mundo", acrescentou John Murray, proprietário da Murray's Tavern.

news_revere.title: Revere to host series of World Cup watch parties
news_revere.author: Oscar Margain
news_revere.summary:
Leaders in Revere, Massachusetts, announced plans for the city to host a series of watch parties and events during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, aiming to draw visitors with an accessible and community-focused alternative to nearby Boston.

City officials planned nine events spread across the 39 days of the tournament as part of “Field of Play,” with locations including Revere Beach, Suffolk Downs, Shirley Avenue and Harry Della Russo Stadium.

Mayor Patrick Keefe Jr. acknowledged the competitive spirit with Boston.

“Everything’s a competition in Revere,” he said. “Of course!”

Keefe said the city’s proximity to Logan Airport and public transportation, along with its dining scene, positioned it as an attractive destination.

“We are six minutes from Logan Airport. We have [MBTA] Blue Line stops that come right from downtown Boston. We have some of the best cuisine and hospitality in the region,” he said.

Revere officials said the city’s diverse population — including large Colombian and Moroccan communities — would play a key role in attracting visitors and creating a unique atmosphere.

“For us, it’s about the long-term folks that visit this community. They walk into a restaurant for the first time. That’s maybe a great experience. They may come back, repeat customers,” said Don Martelli, president of the Revere Chamber of Commerce.

Organizers said the watch parties would also make the tournament more accessible for residents who cannot afford match tickets or transportation costs.

“It’s so expensive. But I tell you, nothing is better than watching the party with the community,” said organizer Rachid Moukhabir, a native of Morocco.

Local fans said the events would bring communities together.

“Soccer is in our blood,” said Samira Jarir, a Moroccan fan from Revere. “To be together and have a lot of fun.”

Others said they still planned to attend matches despite the cost. Woudy Berthelemy of Haiti lives in Lynn and won’t miss seeing his national team for the world, after last participating over 50 years ago.

“I think I’m going to watch my country, whatever the price. I’m going to go,” he said.

The city allocated about $96,000 for the events and said it welcomed additional business partnerships.

“At the end of the day, Revere will be the champ,” Martelli said.

Officials also planned to host a watch party for the World Cup final at Revere Beach, coinciding with the city’s annual sand sculpting festival. Organizers said there would be no registration required and no strict capacity limits.
